title: Word problems with "more" and "fewer" 2
description: Practice solving more word problems by finding how many more (or fewer) objects there are. Numbers used are 20 or less.

assessment_items.question_content:
Some frogs were dancing on a log. $7$ frogs jumped into the pond. There were $2$ frogs left on the log.

**How many frogs were dancing on the log in the beginning? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  frogs
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9357859d74dbe13de4673153117b52a8.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink\Box - \green7 = \blue2$

We can also add all of the frogs together:  $\green7 + \blue2 = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Some monkeys put on a magic show. $4$ of them took a break. $7$ continued the show.

**How many monkeys began performing the show? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  monkeys
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/20ade87c59244635ce49b0a791cc68ae.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green{4} = \blue{7}$

We can also add all of the monkeys together:  $\green{4} + \blue{7} = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There was a plate full of brownies. Tina ate $4$ of the brownies. There were $16$ brownies left.

**How many brownies were on the plate before Tina ate some? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  brownies
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39f76d24cab23d43f6043b43f1766a67.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green{4} = \blue{16}$

We can also add all of the brownies together:  $\green{4} + \blue{16} = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A group of circus bears made a pyramid. $3$ bears fell off the top, leaving $3$ on the bottom.

**How many bears made the pyramid?  **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] bears
hints:
We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green3 = \blue3$

We can also add all of the bears together:  $\green3 + \blue3 = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
AJ bought some magic beans from the store. He dropped $2$ on his way home. When he got home, he had $8$ left.

**How many magic beans did AJ buy? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  beans
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/55cd10d64201f978898f32c37c82ceca.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green{2} = \blue{8}$

We can also add all of the beans together:  $\green{2} + \blue{8} = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Some monster trucks were racing in a rally. $2$ crashed and only $4$ finished the race.

**How many trucks started the race?  **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] trucks
hints:
We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green2 = \blue4$

We can also add all of the cars trucks:  $\green2 + \blue4 = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There was a group of bunnies in a field. $8$ hopped away. Now there are $5$ bunnies in the field.

**How many bunnies were in the group to begin with? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  bunnies
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8d66723ef0eeb3622d88c062d3ef0493.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green8 = \blue5$

We can also add all of the bunnies together:  $\green8 + \blue5 = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There was a group of unicorns talking in a field. $7$ of them ran away. $9$ of them were left.

**In total, how many unicorns were talking in a field? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  unicorns
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/94557dcecda32926d41c2b9e1160f0b6.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green{7} = \blue9$

We can also add all of the unicorns together:  $\green{7} + \blue9 = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A group of witches flew around the city. $5$ of them landed, and there were $12$ left in the sky.

**How many witches flew around the city? **

[[☃ input-number 1]]  witches
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c11cfa2b60eafd219c1d24a61de45cfb.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing whole: $\pink{\Box} - \green{5} = \blue{12}$

We can also add all of the witches together:  $\green{5} + \blue{12} = \pink{\Box}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Sue's ice cream cone has $6$ more scoops than Tessa's ice cream cone.

Sue's ice cream cone has $9$ scoops.

**How many scoops are on Tessa's ice cream cone?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] scoops
hints:
We can take away $\pink{6}$ scoops from the $\purple{9}$ scoops $\purple{\text{Sue}}$ has to see how many scoops $\green{\text{Tessa}}$ has.

$\purple{9} - \pink{6} = \green{\Box}$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24ff7fdff8c7b5286dac8e343fe51742.png)![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb248a1c59d95e55700e97930882ae9c.png)![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d716e16f23b99f43c5dc45fa1186aa46.png)
Tessa's ice cream cone has $\green{3}$ scoops.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fox and squirrel are going down the slides.  Fox chose the long slide, which is $8$ meters long.  The long slide is $2$ meters longer than the short slide.

**How long is the short slide?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a7c8db0399cf8e745507e11c2a0f329.png)
hints:
The short slide is $\green{6}$ meters long.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f72f35be8529605974fee73722de395.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Anna is packing some art supplies to use during a long car trip.  She has $6$ more crayons than colored pencils.  Anna has $14$ crayons.

**How many colored pencils does she have?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] colored pencils

The zoo has $14$ penguins.  There are $6$ more penguins than lions.

**How many lions are at the zoo?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] lions

Farmer Brown is not sure why his horses keep disappearing.  He had $11$ horses yesterday.  He had $3$ more horses yesterday than he has today.

**How many horses are there today?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] horses

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0d561efc92af169578b3afbf1d139e1a.png)
$\qquad\qquad\qquad\qquad \text{Yesterday}$

Harry was driving through town and saw $9$ red traffic lights.  He saw $2$ more red traffic lights than green ones.

**How many green traffic lights did Harry see?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] green traffic lights

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/47cb95c161d1405958df424433383bb8.png)

A giant has an aquarium full of sharks and whales.  There are $5$ more whales than sharks.  There are  $13$ whales in the aquarium.

**How many sharks are in the aquarium?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] sharks

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d0390d82f17e417aea42de4b5a83b7d.png)
hints:
There are $\green{8}$ sharks in the aquarium.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8d0c4dd63bd94712b7d8326e20958487.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Lea has $9$ roly-polies in her bug house.  She has $1$ more than Don.  

**How many roly-polies does Don have?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] roly-polies

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cc6a99ada9dfa0c38eb9b9da9337dec4.png)

There are $4$ more books on the top shelf, shown below, than on the bottom shelf .

**How many books are on the bottom shelf?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 1]] books

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea1a0c623f4bd74aa4c74a7269b5b53a.png)
hints:
We can take away $\pink{4}$ books from the $\purple{7}$ on the $\purple{\text{top shelf}}$ to see how many are on the $\green{\text{bottom shelf}}$.

$\purple{7} - \pink{4} = \green{\Box}$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2cb337128c5c255bbf594599e776baee.png)
There are $\green{3}$ books on the bottom shelf.

assessment_items.question_content:
Raj and Carlos are reading.  Carlos reads a chapter in $4$ minutes, which is $3$ minutes less time than it takes Raj.

**How long does it take Raj to read a chapter?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/527d559a9e601e4cc705e74c0331e07e6302dad0)

Carlos takes $\pink{3\text{ less}}$ minutes than Raj.
Raj takes $\pink{3\text{ more}}$ minutes than Carlos.

We can take the $\blue{4\text{ minutes}}$ Carlos reads and add an extra $\pink{3 \text{ minutes}}$ to get the time Raj reads.

$\blue{4} + \pink{3} = \purple{7}$
It takes Raj $\purple{7}$ minutes to read a chapter.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ann and Zoe love ice cream with cherries on top.  Ann put $4$ cherries on hers.  Ann had $2$ fewer cherries than Zoe.

**How many cherries did Zoe have?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] cherries

The flag of China, shown below, is red with $5$ stars.  It has $2$ fewer stars than the flag of Tajikistan.

**How many stars are on the flag of Tajikistan?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] stars

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1c88418e044e47235e5ca275c051b8b5.png)
hints:
$\green{\text{Stars on China's flag}}+\pink{2}=\purple{\text{Stars on Tajikistan's flag}}$

$\green{5} + \pink{2} =\purple\Box$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1c88418e044e47235e5ca275c051b8b5.png) $\qquad$ ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9bd8b287e424ee8f967eb03ee39b0862.png)
There are $\purple{7}$ stars on the flag of Tajikistan.

assessment_items.question_content:
Hunter found $3$ fewer eggs than his sister Vera.  Hunter found $4$ eggs.

**How many eggs did Vera find?**
  
[[☃ input-number 1]] eggs

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/206dfd53e7a9397c12b3cff1b57e5a5e.png)

The mass of the toy shark is $4$ kilograms less than a red fox.  The mass of the toy shark is $1$ kilogram.  

**What is the mass of the red fox?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/87bd56c3a83241c1b6d2593112591244.png)
hints: The mass of the $\red{\text{red fox}}$ is $\red{5}$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
Javier went to the zoo today and saw $6$ monkeys. That is $3$ fewer than he saw last Saturday. 

**How many monkeys did Javier see last Saturday?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] monkeys

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f3df0bde948a7908801dbd04b6ccaa1e.png)

The Rockers made a CD with $9$ songs on it.  Their CD had $4$ fewer songs than the CD made by Dustin Beemer. 

**How many songs are on Dustin Beemer's CD?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] songs

Jack swam $8$ laps.  He swam $6$ fewer laps than Lea.  

**How many laps did Lea swim?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] laps
